title: Miconia monantha
intro: Miconia monantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "mountain silver tree". This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Miconia monantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Miconia monantha is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into creams and salves for topical applications.
taxonomy: Miconia monantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is commonly known as the melastome family, which consists of about 170 genera and 5,000 species. The Melastomataceae family is part of the order Myrtales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Lythraceae and Combretaceae. Miconia monantha is a member of the tribe Miconieae, which is one of the largest and most diverse tribes within the family Melastomataceae. The specific classification of Miconia monantha is further divided into the genus Miconia and the species M. monantha.
